reasoning: The US has consistently tightened export controls on AI technologies. While a major update was implemented in January 2025, there is a strong possibility of another update within the May-December 2025 timeframe, particularly regarding cloud access (IaaS) for AI computing. A Notice of Proposed Rulemaking on IaaS restrictions was issued in January 2024, distinct from the chip and model weight controls, and finalizing such rules typically takes 1-2 years, placing the likely implementation window firmly in mid-to-late 2025. However, since the January 2025 regulations are still being digested (with compliance required by May 15, 2025), the US might delay further regulations until 2026 to assess the impact, keeping the probability moderate.
